label: Before you start
description: Prepare users before they begin a section of the service. Let them know what they need, how much time is required, and any eligibility conditions that could affect their ability to continue.
purpose: Set accurate expectations upfront to reduce abandonment and prevent avoidable errors later in the journey.
use_when: After a service sheet, before the user begins a complex section.
When new requirements or instructions are introduced mid-journey.
do_not_use_when: For general introduction, promotional, or marketing content.
When the content does not help users prepare for the specific journey that follows.

outputs.label: User acknowledged prerequisites
outputs.description: True when the user has read the preparation content and chosen to proceed.

ux_considerations.text: This step must be read-only and must not ask users for information.
Use a clear title such as 'Before you start' so users immediately understand its purpose.
List required documents or information in plain, specific language, clearly marking anything optional.
Include a single primary action to proceed (e.g. 'Start now' or 'Continue').

technical_notes.text: Prerequisites listed here should be machine-readable so agents can verify readiness before initiating a service flow.
